stories.title: A fragile ceasefire and emboldened Iran
stories.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with International correspondent Daniel Estrin about how the US-Iran-Israel ceasefire is being received in Israel.

stories.title: How Trump's foreign policy is playing in North Carolina
stories.description: What do people who follow foreign affairs make of the war with Iran and the president's foreign policy? NPR spoke with a dozen World Affairs Council members in North Carolina to find out.

stories.title: How did Pakistan bring Iran and the U.S. to the negotiating table?
stories.description: Pakistan has acted as a mediator between Washington D.C. and Tehran over the last few weeks, and helped broker the ceasefire agreement this week. NPR's Juana Summers talks to Elizabeth Threlkeld, a senior fellow and director at the Stimson Center, about why Pakistan decided to st...

stories.title: Iran-U.S. ceasefire off to a shaky start. And, Bill Gates to testify in Epstein probe
stories.description: Attacks persist across the Middle East despite the two-week ceasefire deal between the U.S. and Iran. And, Bill Gates is set to testify in the investigation of the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

stories.title: Israel vows to continue fighting Hezbollah but agrees to talks with Lebanon
stories.description: Israel's prime minister said his government would begin talks with Lebanon but vowed to continue attacks against the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, despite a fragile Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: Trump and NATO chief meet to discuss the partnership, attacks continue across the Gulf region despite U.S.-Iran ceasefire, Iran says ceasefire includes Lebanon, but Israel and Trump say it's not.

stories.title: Iran expert discusses whether the war has made the Iranian regime stronger
stories.description: Iran expert Vali Nasr of Johns Hopkins University discusses whether the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran has made the Iranian regime stronger.

stories.title: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
stories.description: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

stories.title: 'Endless fears': Even if fighting stops, the damage to Iran's children will endure
stories.description: The BBC has been able to obtain testimony from parents and those trying to help children deal with the distress that comes with war.

stories.title: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.description: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.title: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
stories.description: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

stories.title: Israeli strikes in Lebanon 'grave violation' of ceasefire, Iran minister tells BBC
stories.description: Iran's deputy foreign minister says the US must choose between 'war and ceasefire'.

stories.title: Ships remain cautious approaching Strait of Hormuz amid fragile ceasefire
stories.description: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

stories.title: Negotiators face huge task to close gaps in rival Iran peace proposals
stories.description: The US has a 15-point plan to end the war, with Iran offering 10 proposals of its own. Can they be reconciled?

stories.title: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.description: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.title: Israel backs US-Iran ceasefire but Netanyahu's war goals remain unfulfilled
stories.description: The Israeli prime minister's response to the ceasefire has been muted, in contrast to the triumphal statements from the US and Iran.

stories.title: Why ceasefire deal with US has unsettled Iran's hardliners
stories.description: The two-week truce opens up the prospect of direct talks with the US but has angered Iran's hardliners, writes BBC News Persian’s Kasra Naji.

stories.title: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.description: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.title: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Middle East live: Vance heads to Pakistan for Iran talks as Israeli assault on Lebanon tests truce
stories.description: US Vice President JD Vance sets off Friday for Pakistan to lead mediated talks with Iran in a bid to find a resolution to the war as massive Israeli strikes on Lebanon this week tested a fragile US-Iran ceasefire. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu authorised direct negoti...

stories.title: Netanyahu greenlights Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington amid ceasefire tensions
stories.description: Israel and Lebanon are set to hold talks in Washington next week as tensions escalate over continued fighting in Lebanon and concerns grow that the fragile US-Iran ceasefire could collapse. The meeting comes amid heavy strikes, rising casualties, and renewed disputes over the sco...

stories.title: High fuel prices spur interest in EVs
stories.description: The war in Iran has sent fuel costs soaring, and consumers scrambling for savings. At the New York Auto Show, many drivers are warming up to the idea of buying an electric vehicle, because of high petrol prices, as our correspondent Jessica Le Masurier reports. Also in the show -...

stories.title: Sidelined by Trump’s truce with Iran, Israel pummels Lebanon – then agrees to talks
stories.description: The ceasefire between the US and Iran has sidelined Israel and left its war goals largely unmet, leading Benjamin Netanyahu’s critics to lament a “strategic failure” that has damaged Israel’s standing in Washington. With his hands tied on Iran, the Israeli PM has agreed to peace ...

stories.title: Who profits off Iran war? Strait of Hormuz toll talk fuels outrage
stories.description: A most confusing Wednesday it was. In the space of six minutes during the same press conference, the White House spokesperson first hailed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz then called on Iran to immediately reopen the planet’s most oil sensitive shipping lane. Five weeks of ...

stories.title: Macron set for first meeting with Pope Leo amid calls for peace in Middle East
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is set to have his first face-to-face meeting with Pope Leo XIV in the Vatican Friday as the leader of the Roman Catholic church has criticised his fellow American, US President Donald Trump, for "unacceptable belligerence" during the Iran war. Ma...

stories.title: Calls to invoke the 25th Amendment after Trump's Iran threats 'useless'
stories.description: US President Donald Trump’s threat to destroy “a whole civilisation” in Iran has reignited calls to invoke the 25th Amendment and drawn condemnation across the political spectrum. Yet despite a growing chorus of critics, from Democrats to former MAGA allies, the mechanism remains...

stories.title: 'Trump abandoned us': Ceasefire sparks anger, fear, division among Iranians
stories.description: Iranians are divided between relief, disappointment, fear and anger after the announcement of a two-week ceasefire and possible peace plan between the USA and Israel on one side, and Iran on the other. The FRANCE 24 Observers team talked to several Iranians inside Iran to capture...

stories.title: Trump sending US VP Vance to Pakistan for Iran talks
stories.description: US President Donald Trump is sending Vice President JD Vance and other negotiators to the Pakistani capital for talks with Iran that will begin on Saturday, the White House said. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 42 of US-Israeli attacks?
stories.description: A Pakistan-brokered US-Iran ceasefire has taken effect but disputes over its scope are fuelling regional tensions.

stories.title: Kuwait blames Iran for drone strike, Iran denies responsibility
stories.description: Kuwait is blaming ‘Iran and its proxies’ for a drone attack on Thursday night, while Iran has denied responsibility.

stories.title: UK PM Keir Starmer visits Gulf to shore up ‘fragile’ US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: As UK PM Keir Starmer arrived in Doha as part of a Gulf tour he discussed efforts to secure the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: Shipping in Strait of Hormuz at a standstill despite US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: Washington and Tehran accuse each other of not honouring truce agreement.

stories.title: Iran war live: Israeli attacks on Lebanon threaten US-Iran ceasefire talks
stories.description: Iran’s parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warns ‘time is running out’ amid Israel’s attacks on Lebanon.

stories.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei says ‘we seek compensation, not war’
stories.description: Iranian state TV has read out a message from new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei which says Iran ‘does not want war’.

stories.title: US-Iran ceasefire: Uncertainty over what’s next
stories.description: Two‑week US‑Iran ceasefire pauses war; “who won?” unresolved as Hormuz, Lebanon strikes, Iran capabilities persist.

stories.title: Tears of joy mark reopening of Jerusalem holy sites after 41 days
stories.description: • Worshippers describe an ‘indescribable happiness’, one calls it ‘a miracle’• Tensions flare as police disperse Muslims at Al Aqsa for Jewish visitors JERUSALEM : Just before 5am at Jerusalem’s Al Aqsa Mosque compound, a Muslim worshipper prayed in tears. A few hours later, simi...

stories.title: Pakistan allows visa-free boarding, offers visa on arrival for Islamabad Talks delegates
stories.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar on Friday said airlines have been instructed to allow visa-free boarding for individuals travelling to the country in connection with the US-Iran talks in Islamabad. Foreign delegations from Iran and the US are expected in Isla...

stories.title: Britain accuses Russia of ‘covert’ op in North Atlantic
stories.description: LONDON: Britain said on Thursday it had tracked and deterred three Russian submarines on an alleged month-long “covert operation” in UK waters in the North Atlantic near vital undersea cables and pipelines. Disclosing details of the joint mission with Norway and other unspecified...

stories.title: US forces to remain deployed near Iran until ‘real’ agreement reached, says Trump
stories.description: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump vowed on Thur­sday to retain military assets in the Middle East until a peace deal with Iran is reached and warned of a major escalation in fighting if Iran failed to comply. Trump said in a social media post that US ships, aircraft, and pers...

stories.title: Hormuz traffic plummets as Iran asserts control
stories.description: • Global oil supply cut by 20pc; backlog of hundreds of ships grows• Truce brings little relief as security firms warn of persistent risks LONDON: Ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz stood at well below 10 per cent of normal volumes on Thursday despite a US-Iran ceasefire a...

stories.title: In written message, Iran supreme leader says Tehran does not seek war but will not renounce legitimate rights
stories.description: Iran’s new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei in his latest written message said that the Islamic republic does not want war with the United States and Israel, but would protect its rights as a nation, state television reported on Thursday. “We did not seek war and we do not want it...

stories.title: Israel seeks peace talks with Lebanon ‘as soon as possible’ as world condemns Tel Aviv’s bombardment amid US-Iran truce
stories.description: Israel’s Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Thursday he had ordered his ministers to begin direct talks with Lebanon, pushing for Hezbollah’s disarmament amid mounting concern that its ongoing strikes could cause the fragile US-Iran truce to unravel. A Lebanese government ...

stories.title: Iran, Saudi FMs hold first call since start of conflict, discuss ways to reduce tensions
stories.description: Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan received a phone call from his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi, the Saudi foreign ministry said on Thursday. It marked the first official contact between the countries since Tehran launched strikes against its Gulf neighbours in ...

stories.title: Israel agrees to hold talks with Lebanon under US pressure — media
stories.description: According to Israel’s Kan radio station, the talks will focus on establishing a ceasefire and disarming Hezbollah units

stories.title: Islamabad tightens security ahead of Iran-US talks
stories.description: The army and rangers will ensure the protection of the red zone housing government buildings and diplomatic missions

stories.title: US, Iran to fail to make deal, but Washington to get out of war — expert
stories.description: According to Trita Parsi, Tehran considers it "essential" to ensure that the ceasefire regime extends to Lebanon

stories.title: Hezbollah drones attack military facility in northern Israel — Tasnim
stories.description: The strike was delivered in the area of the settlement of Ya’ara

stories.title: Japan needs relations with various oil suppliers, including Russia — expert
stories.description: Tsuneo Watanabe emphasized that the opening of the Strait of Hormuz may not happen soon

stories.title: Shortage of energy supplies from Persian Gulf may last more than year — Japanese expert
stories.description: Tsuneo Watanabe says he does not expect the Strait of Hormuz to be open soon

stories.title: Ceasefire between US and Iran very fragile, Japanese expert says
stories.description: According to Tsuneo Watanabe, it will be difficult for the sides to agree on a number of issues, including Tehran's nuclear program

stories.title: US insists on specific NATO commitments on Strait of Hormuz — CNN
stories.description: According to the report, President Donald Trump insisted that NATO nations take concrete measures in the next few days

stories.title: Advisor to supreme leader of Iran died from wounds received in attack on Tehran — TV
stories.description: The Iranian state television says Kamal Kharazi "died a few hours ago"

stories.title: Trump says Iran should not charge fee for passage through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The US president noted he had received reports that Iran was charging fees to tankers going through the strait

stories.title: Iran won the war despite damage — leader’s statement
stories.description: According to Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran will take the management of the Strait of Hormuz to a new level

stories.title: US, Israel to rearm during ceasefire with Iran — Dutch expert
stories.description: According to Kees van der Pijl, this may indicate a desire by both sides to prepare for a potential continuation of the protracted standoff, even if a short-term pause is in place

stories.title: Ukraine may face a shortage of jet fuel due to conflict over Iran — expert
stories.description: According to the expert, the 20% loss of oil from this region has had a disproportionate impact on the jet fuel market

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
